fixes: Ensure type names appear in type positions and value names appear in value positions

If you intended to reference a value, check that the name resolves to a binding rather than a type or generic parameter

entries.name: Disconnected
entries.description: The connection to the wallet was lost mid-session (e.g. the WebSocket or communication channel dropped). Distinct from a never-connected state.
fixes: Re-establish connection by calling connect(networkId)
Use getConnectionStatus() to observe connection state

entries.name: InternalError
entries.description: An internal error occurred within the dapp connector.
fixes: Check dapp connector logs for details and retry the operation

entries.name: InvalidRequest
entries.description: The DApp sent an invalid or malformed request. Per the spec, this can be thrown in various circumstances — a malformed transaction is one example.
fixes: Review the dapp connector API specification and correct the request format

entries.name: PermissionRejected
entries.description: Session-level / persistent denial — the user has set a general preference to not permit this action for this DApp. Once observed for a particular API method, expect it to keep being returned for the session (until the browser tab/window is closed).
fixes: Inform the user that the DApp is blocked at the wallet-permission level
Direct the user to unblock the DApp in wallet settings if they want to proceed
Do NOT auto-retry — the rejection is persistent for the session

entries.name: Rejected
entries.description: One-time user rejection — the user saw the request (e.g. saw the cost of a transaction) and explicitly declined it.
fixes: Inform the user the action was cancelled
Do not auto-retry; let the user re-initiate

entries.name: JobQueueFull
entries.description: The proof server job queue is full and cannot accept new requests.
fixes: Retry after a short delay
Consider reducing request concurrency

entries.name: JobMissing
entries.description: The requested proof job was not found on the server.
fixes: Verify the job ID is correct
Resubmit the proof request if the job has expired

entries.name: JobNotPending
entries.description: The job cannot be processed because it is not in a pending state.
fixes: Check the job status before attempting to process it

entries.name: BadInput
entries.description: The proof server received invalid input for the proof job.
fixes: Verify the circuit inputs are correctly encoded and within valid ranges

entries.name: InternalError
entries.description: An internal error occurred in the proof server.
fixes: Check proof server logs for details
Retry the request

entries.name: ChannelClosed
entries.description: An internal worker channel was unexpectedly closed in the proof server.
fixes: Restart the proof server
File a bug report if the issue persists

entries.name: Busy
entries.description: The proof server is too busy to accept new requests.
fixes: Retry after backing off
Scale the proof server if load is consistently high

entries.name: Ready
entries.description: The indexer is running and ready to serve requests.

entries.name: BadRequest
entries.description: The indexer received a malformed or invalid request.
fixes: Review the request format and correct any invalid fields or parameters

entries.name: PayloadTooLarge
entries.description: The request payload exceeds the indexer's maximum allowed size.
fixes: Reduce the size of the request payload
Paginate or split large queries

entries.name: ServiceUnavailable

fixes: Wait for the indexer to finish syncing and retry

entries.description: Substrate transaction pool rejected the extrinsic as invalid. The wire-level error has the shape `{code: 1010, message: "Invalid Transaction", data: "Custom error: N"}` where N is the inner LedgerApiError u8. To decode N, look up codes 0-255 in this dataset.
fixes: Read the inner data string — it ends with `Custom error: N` where N is a u8 0-255
Look up that u8 in the Midnight node error tables (e.g. 168 was FeeCalculation, now retired; 155 is FeeCalculationError; 154 is BlockLimitExceededError)

group.description: Errors raised during the ZKIR lowering passes.

entries.description: The ZKIR pass encountered a reference to a built-in circuit that is not registered in this compiler version.
fixes: Confirm the language version pragma matches the installed compiler
Update the compiler to a version that supports the named primitive
If the call is to user-defined code, verify it is not shadowing a future built-in name

entries.code: zkir returned a non-zero exit status
entries.name: ZKIR non-zero exit status

group.name: ZKIR Generation Errors
group.description: Errors raised during the ZKIR lowering passes.

entries.description: The external ZKIR backend exited with a non-zero status. Raised via `external-errorf`, so the compiler exits with the external-error code rather than reporting a source-level diagnostic.
fixes: Inspect the ZKIR backend output for the underlying failure; the exit status alone does not name the unsupported operation
Reduce the offending circuit (often by removing or replacing operations the backend does not yet support) and retry
Confirm the ZKIR toolchain version is compatible with the compiler version in use
aliases: zkir returned a non-zero exit status ~d

entries.name: Malformed contract-info file

group.name: External Contract Reference Errors
group.description: Errors involving external contract metadata, contract-info JSON files, and module imports.

entries.description: The compiler could not parse a `contract-info` JSON file referenced by an external contract declaration. Raised via `external-errorf`, so this exits with the external-error code rather than reporting at a specific source location.
fixes: Recompile the upstream contract — `try recompiling <path>` is suggested in the message
If the file was hand-edited or partially written, delete it and regenerate it via a fresh compile
Verify the upstream contract's compiler version is compatible with the calling contract's compiler
aliases: malformed contract-info file ~a for ~s: ~a; try recompiling ~a

entries.code: has been modified more recently than
entries.name: Stale contract-info file

group.name: External Contract Reference Errors
group.description: Errors involving external contract metadata, contract-info JSON files, and module imports.

entries.description: The Compact source for an external contract is newer than its cached `contract-info` JSON file. The cache is stale and may not reflect the actual contract definition.
fixes: Recompile the upstream contract so the `contract-info` file is regenerated from the current source
If the upstream source was reverted, touch the contract-info file or rebuild to refresh the timestamps
Make recompilation part of the build pipeline so this drift cannot accumulate silently
aliases: ~a has been modified more recently than ~a; try recompiling ~a

entries.name: Contract declaration mismatch

group.name: External Contract Reference Errors
group.description: Errors involving external contract metadata, contract-info JSON files, and module imports.

entries.description: The calling contract's external declaration disagrees with the actual contract definition. The family covers presence (a declared circuit is missing or an extra one is declared), purity (the pure flag does not match), arity (different argument counts), argument types, and return type.
fixes: Update the calling contract's external declarations so argument count, types, return type, purity, and circuit names match the actual definition
Or recompile and update the upstream contract to match the calling contract's declared shape
If the contract has changed, treat this as a breaking change and propagate it to all callers
aliases: contract ~s has no circuit declaration named ~s
contract declaration claims circuit ~s is pure, but it is not in the actual contract definition
contract declaration claims circuit ~s has ~s argument~:*~p, but in the actual contract definition it has ~s
contract declaration claims the type of circuit ~s argument ~s is ~a, but in the actual contract definition it is ~a
contract declaration claims the return type of circuit ~s is ~a, but in the actual contract definition it is ~a
contract declaration has a circuit named ~a, but it is not present in the actual contract definition

entries.code: defines module rather than expected module
entries.name: Module name mismatch

group.name: External Contract Reference Errors
group.description: Errors involving external contract metadata, contract-info JSON files, and module imports.

entries.description: A Compact source file is imported under one module name, but its top-level `module` declaration uses a different name.
fixes: Rename the file's `module` declaration to match the import path, or rename the import to match the file's actual module name
If the file was renamed on disk, update both ends to keep them consistent
Standardize on a single module name per file across the project
aliases: ~a defines module ~s rather than expected module ~s

entries.name: event/log reserved for future use

group.name: External Contract Reference Errors
group.description: Errors involving external contract metadata, contract-info JSON files, and module imports.

entries.description: The identifiers `event` and `log` were added to Compact's reserved-for-future-use keyword group in compiler 0.31.101. Using either as a variable, function, or type name now produces a parse error.
fixes: Rename the identifier — `event` and `log` are reserved for future Compact language features
Choose a more specific name (e.g. `eventName`, `logEntry`) to avoid future reservations

Audit the rest of the file for other reserved-for-future-use names: await, break, case, catch, class, continue, debugger, delete, do, extends, finally, function, implements, in, instanceof, interface, let, null, package, private, protected, public, static, super
aliases: keyword ~s (which is reserved for future use)

entries.code: Uint range end expressed as a reference to generic size
entries.name: Uint range end may need manual update

group.name: Compiler Warnings
group.description: Non-fatal diagnostics emitted via source-warningf.

entries.description: A `Uint` range end is expressed as a reference to a generic size symbol. When the generic size is later bound to a concrete value, the compiler cannot rewrite the range end automatically — the resulting range may be too narrow or too wide for the bound size. This is a deferred warning, surfaced after the size resolution pass.
fixes: After the generic size is resolved, audit Uint range ends that referenced the generic and update them by hand
Replace the generic-size reference with a concrete value if the binding is known at the declaration site
Compilation still succeeds — this is informational, not fatal
aliases: Uint range end expressed as a reference to generic size ~a is left unchanged and must be updated manually

entries.name: Rename not applied (binding-aware rewrite skipped)

group.name: Compiler Warnings
group.description: Non-fatal diagnostics emitted via source-warningf.

entries.description: The compiler attempted a deprecation-driven rename (typically the old standard-library / ledger operator name to its new form) but skipped it because applying the rename would either shadow an existing in-scope binding or cause variable capture.
fixes: Rename the conflicting local binding first, then re-run the compile so the rename can apply
Update the call site manually to the new name if the local binding cannot be moved
Compilation still succeeds — but the reference points at the old (possibly soon-to-be-removed) name
aliases: not renaming reference of ~s to ~s because ~1:*~s has other bindings in scope
not renaming reference of ~s to ~s because ~2:*~s has other bindings in scope
not renaming reference of ~s to ~s because this would cause the reference to be captured by an existing local binding for ~:*~s
